name: Pot-Roasted Salmon with Olives & Preserved Le
recipeInstructions:
Preserved lemons are salted lemons - cut a vertical cross in each lemon and stuff with sea salt. Preserve in lemon juice for a few weeks in an air-tight jar. If unavailable, use ordinary lemons. The advantage of preserved lemons is their mildness and tenderness, allowing them to be eaten raw. Unpreserved ones can be too bitter.
If fish stock is not available, use 3 oz each white wine and water.
1. Brown the salmon in olive oil using a casserole dish for roasting. Season with salt and pepper. Set aside.
2. Peel and trim carrots, then cut into thin strips. Top and tail green beans and roughly chop black olives.
3. In the same pan, lightly color the vegetables in hot oil.
4. Add the black olives and preserved lemon (cut into eight segments) to the oil.
5. Pour over the fish stock. Bring to a boil, then lower the heat. Place the fish on top and cover with the lid and foil if using.
6. Cook over moderate heat for eight minutes, basting the fish twice during the process.
7. Remove the fish and keep warm. Continue cooking vegetables uncovered until tender.
8. Serve with the fish on top and sprinkle chopped parsley.
